step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the majority opinion that the most likely diagnosis is atrial fibrillation (option C). One additional supporting detail is worth noting—the patient being a long-term smoker. Chronic tobacco use not only increases the risk of developing arrhythmias but also contributes to systemic inflammation which might indirectly affect cardiac rhythm stability. Moreover, while multifocal atrial tachycardia (MFT) typically occurs in older adults with chronic lung disorders or electrolyte imbalances, the absence of these factors combined with the strong association between smoking and AFIB strengthens our case for choosing option C.

Final answer: C. Atrial Fibrillation. Your agreement adds another layer of reasoning regarding the impact of chronic tobacco use on cardiac health, reinforcing why atrial fibrillation should remain the top consideration. Final
